reviews.review_text:
This game is pure agony, oh my god. 

I may just be bad at games but a lot of my hours were spent raging. Great game but uuuuurrgh.
Incredibly fun, incredibly hard. Lot of charm and fun lore.

Excellent little game, my favourite roguelike of all time. No matter how much time passes I always come back to it. My only complaint with it is that the rng required for certain achievements and to unlock some of the ships is insane.

played it a ton and its obvious when the game decides that you've lost. the ships you will go against are a direct counter to whatever you've built. use fire? automated enemies with no O2. maxed shields? enemies will have lasers and trash guns that fire 4-6 projectiles a shot. use a teleporter to board enemies? zoltan shields will be sure to appear. using a beam weapon? enemy shields will be maxed. got a fast firing projectile weapon? your weapon system will be hacked and hammered with missiles. using cloak? "random" shots will hit your cloaking station guaranteed every chance they get. using drone weapons? enemies will have anti drones from that point on.

Played this back in 2013 when Angry Joe reviewed it, been coming back to it to play nonstop every now and then to this day whenever I get the urge to blow up enemy ships and use their scrap to get shiny new loadouts to blow up other ships with.

It's a bit of a learning curve, as learning to time your shots to land in just the right way to take out the enemy shields -and- slam your beam into the enemy hull as it goes down massively improves your run just as much as knowing when to flee and what order to buy upgrades in. (hint: get 2 bars of shields first), and while bad or unlucky situations can be unavoidable, getting good enough to mitigate those damages and salvage a run despite horrible odds is an amazing feeling that feels much more achievable to me on the small scale that FTL runs on.

It's not one I can guarantee all my friends would enjoy, but it's my absolute personal favourite
i started playing this game about 2 days ago and i already have 11h on it. Yeah the hours say it all just buy it

omg i love this game. i cant stop playing it 8 years later. i play it like twice a month atleast. i find other games like it them. if you have a steam acount you must play ftl. its a classic master piece that others have tried to copy but will not because of there love for this game or there just dumb.

FTL my beloved

Love-hate relationship with this game, and the game obviously hates me
this game came out like 15 years ago and i still play it like every other month. the ultimate 'plays well on a potato, absolutely destroys a couple of hours' game.

Good systemic game with intense and decision dense combat, but sometimes even if you did nothing wrong - you loose because of bad luck. If you don't mind screwing your run sometimes - totally worth it!
Very cool strategy game, kind of like operating the starship voyager through the delta quadrant

I've played this game for many years (outside steam). This game is an absolute legend of an indie game. It's still just as fun today as it was before. If you feel like you already got the most out of this game, the modding community here is amazing. There is a mod called "Multiverse", that adds hundreds if not thousands of hours of fresh gameplay to enjoy. And it's still being developed to this day!
Genuinely one of the best games that has ever been created. Including the modding scene, you can really pull a LOT of extremely fun playtime out of it.
Really addicting and a real indie classic which inspired a bunch of other games.
great game. very complicated and more then worth the price.

This game has such a steep learning curve that it takes many people hours upon hours to complete easy mode, myself included. I like a challenge, especially in an age where video games have become much easier. This game is easy to run on a computer, has fun gameplay and mechanics, good graphics, and great content. The replayability is extremely good as well. I highly recommend, 10/10.

One of the most challenging and addictive roguelikes you'll come across. Just as likely to make you scream in anger as jump for joy. Been around for a long time but I don't think the format has ever been bettered. 

Fantastic musical score as well and while the graphics may be basic by todays standards, it does have a real charm to them. 

In space the space bar is your only friend, pop the game on Pause often while learning. It's the only way to figure out all the micro managing you need to do, from power management to weapon targets and boarding parties to drone control. A cloak at the right time can save a run as can a well aimed hack of the enemies piloting.

This game will not hold your hand and the final boss is brutal but so satisfying to beat.
It's like single player Dungeons and Dragons in space. I love the randomness of each time I play. Never the same game twice.

This is my favorite space themed game of all time, while its not perfect (sometimes you can get soft-locked, or winning a fight up to pure RNG, though albeit these are not too common) it does a LOT of things right. It's definitely strategy heavy, but its a very engaging and fun kind! There is a lot of micro involved in late game, but being able to pause everything to set up your attacks or crew makes it a lot nicer. Every system and weapon interacts with each other in unique ways, with a lot of different play styles. Overall an amazing game!

Also the sound track is a banger.
This game has a fun gameplay loop with enough difficulty to make it a rewarding experience. The soundtrack is also really well done. I would definitely recommended FTL if you like roguelikes.
It is an interesting strategy game that forces you to think about the best path for each spaceship and the decisions you should make in any given situation

My biggest complaint with this game is that unlocking all the ships is a pain.  I feel like I had some other minor complaints with the game but it has been so long since I last played I don't remember what they were.  This is a great game overall though, I think I'd give it a 8.5~9/10.
I absolutely have no idea how I played this game for 88 hours in 3 days but this is probably the best roguelike out there! Its reall challenging and has a lot of replayability.

Really fun game if you like roguelikes and 2-D space combat. If you get FTL, just remember; the game is preparing for the Flagship fight. Sometimes not spending scrap is the best play, and if you're diving then spending that scrap is usually the best course of action.

never have i seen a game with so much ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ in it
my ship died at 1 jump from the final sector because i took some dude in my ship that ended up sabotaging it and destroying it right at the exit
survived an entire system at 1 hull point, explored EVERY SINGLE star in the system and there wasnt a single store in sight, died because i ended up at a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ solar flare
help the guy being chased that wants to hide in your ship? he sabotages it. side with the people hunting them? believe it or not, you get your ship sabotaged
some ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ surrendered, i accepted it, but their guys boarding my ship just decided they didnt hear no bell and kept killing my crew, pretty sure that's against the geneva convention
lost 3 of my crewmembers back to back in unrelated events, then the last guy decided to just become evil for some reason so that ended the game since i technically didnt have anyone left
overall the most ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ crazy things will continually punch you in the face at every step of the way
and yet i keep coming back every time, for some reason

There is nothing more fun, when most of your ship is burning, and the flagship is also near death. The whole screen is full of warnings, and the flagship is about to use its power surge. But in the final moments, you manage to fire your weapons, and defeat the flagship. I remember playing this game 8 years ago when I didn't even understood English.
Some good things:
 -Great art
 -Amazing music
 -Insane replay value
 -Interesting worldbuilding 
 -Mods (Multiverse is THE largest mod which I ever seen for any game)
And some bad:
 -Sometimes it feels like I lose (or win) due to RNG
 -Can be really hard sometimes
 -Giant alien spiders
I suggest starting on easy difficulty, even normal is really hard early on.
As a final advice: Giant alien spiders are no joke!
10/10
This game is really good for what it is. It's management and coziness, at the same time.
FTL: Faster Than Light is a Cornerstone of videogames. I am happy that this was one of my first games on Steam
I love this game! I really like the fact that it takes ages to unlock all ships and the advanced editon content is super cool.
This is one of the greatest games of all time. Thank you Dan of Nerd Cubed for showing me this masterpiece.

The game is an absolute blast. Gameplay can be repetitive but overall its a fun loop and its different almost every single time. Most of my gameplay has been spent unlocking the various ships of which there are many and it can actually be quite the challenge. Overall a very fun roguelike.

excelent game, if you want more content look into the FTL: Multiverse mod
dont forget to mute coms and jump away

A benchmark game. A gold standard of roguelikes.

Some runs the game hands you a win on a platter, some runs the game wants you to lose. As you get better, you'll find it harder to come across 'unwinnable' runs. 

Seriously why are you reading reviews for a nearly 14yr old indie game that people STILL reference as a titan of the genre? Just play it.

This is an ABSLOUTLY AMAZING game. I have 100%ed the game on my ipad and keep coming back. there is a LOT to learn before things start to become easy. i do know that there is a verry big RNG aspect to the game that can soft lock a run. my biggest suggestion to fix that problem is to add the "Multiverse" mod many of the issues that can hold you back on a good run have been fixed. Plus it adds hundreds of new ships, classes, weapons, events, back grounds, songs and so much more that it is almost a new game.
I think this may have been one of the first rouglike games I ever played and I absolutely recommend this amazing game. Only reservation is that I wish this game is more steamdeck friendly.
Over 10 years old, Ive owned it for over half that time, and I still play it from time to time. Excellent game with excellent replay-ability.
You think you're prepared, go against the final boss, get obliterated, game over. 10/10, would false sense of confidence again.

A really nice game to float around inside and just forget about the world for a bit, there is this urge to do "just another one" and another one... Perfect to have by your side even if you are studying or doing something else because you can always pause the game.

Every round is like its own little adventure, I think that the devs here really did a nice job! Worth the money if you are like me and like some fun indie games that can take you on an adventure =)
Very simple and entertaining game. Only recommendation would be for more ways to farm resources

One of my favorite games to come back to time and time again, its so easy to pick up and start a run, and it always feels different.  So many different ships and weapons and strategies to keep me playing more and more, each ship having its own starting abilities and weapons as wells as challenges to unlock new variants of the ship with their own layouts. 

Theres a lot going on here. 

After enjoying all the content the base game offers, I highly recommend looking into FTL Multiverse mod, which adds so much content to the game its unreal.
Great game. I'm still playing it off and on after 14 years ... that should be enough of a recommendation.
Fun and challenging game that you can play for hours highly recommend

The quintessential "one more run" game. Procedurally generated space encounters keep every playthrough fresh and tense. Managing your crew and ship systems under pressure is incredibly satisfying. Simple to learn, nearly impossible to master, and endlessly replayable.

Highly recomend
This is literally my favorit game of all time, and if you are even slightly into tactical roguelikes it may be yours to

After 40 hours in FTL, I can confidently say this is one of the greatest strategy games ever made. Every single run feels like its own desperate sci-fi story where you’re barely holding the ship together while making impossible decisions every jump. The amount of tension this game creates with such simple mechanics is honestly incredible. One minute you feel unstoppable, the next your oxygen is gone, your engines are on fire, and your last crew member is fighting giant alien spiders in the medbay.

The replayability is insane. Even after dozens of hours I’m still discovering new events, strategies, ship builds, and ridiculous ways to lose. The soundtrack is absolutely legendary and somehow makes every battle feel ten times more intense. The art style is simple but iconic, and the atmosphere is unmatched. Few games make victories feel this rewarding because every win feels earned.

FTL is the perfect example of “easy to learn, impossible to master.” It’s brutal, unfair, stressful, and completely addictive in the best possible way. If you enjoy strategy, roguelikes, or sci-fi at all, this is a must-play masterpiece. Easily one of the best purchases I’ve ever made on Steam.
Most difficult game even on Easy.  Most of the times cannot even get out of second map. I wish Easy setting would be really easy, not tryhard.
I don't typically enjoy Roguelike games, but this one is the exception.

its crazy how whenever Im out of games to play, I start FTL and realize...this game actually great.
But jesus even after 70h playtime its still a rough game.

>start game
>get to sector 5 with federation ship
>explode
>use engi ship
>unlock zoltan ship
>explode
>use zoltan ship
>max out every upgrade 
>get to flagship
>get hit by 5 shots in a row
>explode
10/10 game i recommend

this game scares me
i like it !!

not casual material i don't think. even easy mode is barely beatable, to the point where it feels unfair sometimes. but if you like thinking :) and being in constant fear, this game is great
music is great too, ty Ben Punty

incredibly fun and incredibly difficult
i won't get filtered !! maybe
Attack all slavers. Not for any moral reason but because if you get them low they give you a free crewmember (slave).

When I was around 12 I would play this game on my other account and remembered being so bad at it, I always told myself I did not understand it and that it was a good game, I was just bad.

4 years ago I got this game again on this account to see if I got any better since I am more used too this type of game and I have to say, This game is just Yatzy. Go play Yatzy, The skill of "I guess I should count it at this since I might not get it later" Is the same skill as playing this game. 

Visualy: This game looks fantastic and not dated at all
Music: Fantastic OST that I still listen too sometimes in the car
Story and Choices: A little limited and repetitive but good for what you are getting

Gameplay?: Random ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. You could do everything perfectly but if you take a single risk in a new weapon, Ya dead. Got shot early with the first ship? Ya dead. If you like Gambling please get this game, Because you could not take a single lick of Dmg, Perfectly getting loads of Scrap to get more Sheild and ONE FIGHT is enough to loose. 

FTL is ok. Just ok. If you want a game that rewards you for skill and managment do not get FTL.

You will hate yourself for buying it. Only reason I keep playing is because I already hate myself in the first place. This is sometimes very rarely maybe fun once in a while possibly if your idea of fun is dragging your genitals through broken glass and lit coals. If you are having fun the RNG will do everything it can to destroy that fun if you don't believe me go ahead and buy it.

played then it was free, completed few runs, boughted, played again, recommend

spaceship simulation? roguelike-like? strategy? - i like it
pros: have some fun achievments
cons: to get new ships u have to play a lot
Awesome space ship management game. Tons of re-playability!
always support subset  games their amazing and i hope they continue making new ones

Me pick crew. Me manage ship.
Fire spreads. I panic. Airlocks vent. Suffocation creeps. 
Shields down. Hull breached. Mantis boarding.
Permadeath hits. I lose everything.
Start over. Learn. Adapt. Win.

AI brutal. Fair. Honest.
Every death teaches me something.
Every run feels different.
Replayability endless.

Conclusion:
Game good.
Concept excellent.
Roguelike perfection.
Play it!
ftl: faster than light causes great damage to the human spirit without a single benefit

This what Adama always do in BSG.

Standby FTL, JUMP! or... your entire fleet will get destroy.
14 years later still the goat. Subset games please come back.
this is a game where the difficulty ruined alot of the actual fun of the game
Very little hidden information from the player. High difficulty. High variance between attempt.
best rougelike ever, multiverse mod is just as good with more content
Fun and is sci fi for the win. you need to keep pushing ahead in this one there's no halfway try!
This is still my favorite singleplayer game of all time.
The game is challenging and a lot of fun! I love it
goated Love da soundtrack, Very difficult, I liek
fun if you like easy mode to feel like an almost impossible challenge.
Great game. Adding mods make it more replayable
Amazingly compelling little spaceship game
If I had Steam and money when I was 12-13, this banger of game would've had easily 1.5k+ hours....
doesnt work half the time, by the way. ill just pirate a version because those work :)
awesome early entry in the rogue like genre. Holds up.
I forced my friend to play and now he is addicted, 10/10.
This game is extremely hard but also fun
Cool management game make brain juices go brrr
The problem of being faster than light is that you can only live in darkness
nice and addicting
I wish this game got another content update. It's so good
good game, fps issues
It's just really tight and good.
I've sunk almost 100 hours into this game. Still fun.
Difficult but fun
The game becomes so broken once you get flak guns
pretty perfect game, I have loved it for ages
Simple, but engaging.
Very chill, very repeatable.
Decent enough for the price.
